pages.raw_text:
Former indian Agent a. NJ An
derson was acquitted by a Jury in
the federal court last evening of a
charge of perjury growing out Of
the transfer to the government of
some Indian lands. The case was
holly contested for three days, the
government having brought wit
nesses from Washington D. C. The
jury was out but Is minutes.


A denial that he treated IT'S wife
cruelty is made by Martin McCon-
ahy, defendant in a divorce case.
He says that he Is blinded in one
eye, aged and decrepit and there
fore unable physically to abuse his
wife.


The E. Goldberg residence, at
the southeast corner of Seven
teenth and Grand in Mantle, has
been purchased by Senator r. A..
Hutchinson for $4,000. The house
was built about a year ago and is
of the colonial type.


An immense irrigation project at
Post Falls has been floated by John
S.. Malloy of Malloy Bros, who has
interested Chicago capitalists. An
area of $500 acres will be put under
Irrigation with water pumped from
Hayden Lake, four miles distant.
The enterprise, it is announced,
will represent on completion an in
vestment of 100000.


General James A. Drain, presi-
dent of the national militia board,
is in the city. General Drain still
calls Spokane home, and will re-
turn here to live after he completes
his work at the national capital.


Judge HinkIe yesterday severed
the ties that held three unhappy
couples. Women were the plain
tiffs in each case.


Justice Stocker lost no time when
Walter L. Wright of Spokane and
Dorothea Monkey of Pueblo, Col,
appeared before him with a mar
riage license, but tied them up on
the spot. Justice Stocker is recog-
nized as the boss marrying squire
of Spokane county.


The membership Of Spokane's Y.
M. c. A. is almost as large aslthat
of Seattle. The Spokane assecia-
ton has 2207, while Seattlesihas
1920.


Gustaf Gustafson, an epileptic,
and Frank Smith, a victim of alco-
holism, were adjddged insane by
Judge Hfnkle.


Alleging that Deputy Prosecutor
Kizer asked him if he belonged to
the BLACK hand" society, thereby
prejudicing his case before 7lthe
jury, Rocco Catalano, convicted Of
shooting at Attilio Mesemrio,s has
applied to Judge Webster for ANEW
trial.
